exemplar.title: How has the academy's growth at Sporting Clube de Portugal affected its competitiveness and financial sustainability over the last ten years?

detailedExemplarData.title: How has the academy's growth at Sporting Clube de Portugal affected its competitiveness and financial sustainability over the last ten years?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated with defined timeframe and measurable dimensions
Topic is communicated adequately with sufficient contextual explanation
Methodology identifies a generally relevant range of secondary sources and analytical tools
weaknesses: Explanation remains largely descriptive without comprehensive definitions of key terms
Methodology lacks critical evaluation of source reliability and detailed procedures for data collection
Discussion occasionally drifts from the research question, reducing focus and coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The strand assessments consistently placed Focus and Method in the mid band (3â4), with clear communication of topic and question but notable gaps in definitions, rationale depth, and methodological rigour. Given the descriptive nature of the introduction and the lack of critical justification in the methods section, a score at the lower end of the band (3) accurately reflects performance here.

strengths: Demonstrates a sound grasp of business management concepts such as human capital theory, ROI, and Porterâs strategies
Use of key terminology is mostly accurate and consistent
Sources are applied in a way that generally supports discussion of competitiveness and sustainability
weaknesses: Application of source material often reproduces data without rigorous interrogation
Some calculations contain methodological flaws or rely on limited data points
Citations are occasionally vague, reducing the clarity of source use
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand scores ranged from 2 (limited source application) to 3 (clear knowledge and terminology use), indicating work that is generally coherent but lacks depth in data interrogation and precision in calculations. A mid-band score of 3 reflects solid conceptual understanding tempered by weaknesses in rigorous application.

strengths: Research is clearly linked to the research question with a reasonable spread of financial and external data
Analysis applies frameworks (e.g., Porterâs) and quantitative tools (ROI, costâbenefit) to interpret findings
Conclusions are coherent and supported by the presented data
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely descriptive and does not challenge underlying assumptions
Limited source triangulation and absence of primary perspectives reduce evaluation depth
Evaluation of alternative explanations and external factors is brief and uncritical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work falls in the 4â6 band for Critical Thinking, showing clear analysis and generally relevant research but lacking the sophistication and critical evaluation for the top half of the band. A score of 5 reflects competent analytical work with room for deeper interrogation of assumptions, broader triangulation, and more critical evaluation.

strengths: Includes all basic structural elements (title page, table of contents, page numbers, word count)
Uses headings, tables, and figures with captions to support readability
weaknesses: Formatting is uneven: figure scaling, table borders, and citation styles are inconsistent
Section ordering sometimes interrupts narrative flow
Legends, axis labels, and narrative introductions to figures are often missing or unclear
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is serviceable but lacks the polish and consistency required for the top band (3â4). The presence of required elements and captions merits the upper half of the 1â2 band, giving a score of 2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is clearly statedââHow has the academyâs growth at Sporting Clube de Portugal affected its competitiveness and financial sustainability over the last ten years?ââand it sets a defined time frame and two measurable dimensions (competitiveness, financial sustainability). Nonetheless, the essay occasionally drifts into general club history or broad comparisons with Ajax and Barcelona without always tying these sections back to the RQ. This partial loss of focus means the connection between question and discussion is only moderate, placing the strand in the middle band.

The student proposes that academy development is the âonly wayâ for revenue growth and outlines limitations regarding data availability. However, evaluation of alternative strategies, external factors (e.g., macro-economic conditions, competitive dynamics) and the robustness of data is brief and largely uncritical. Arguments are presented but tend to confirm preconceived views rather than interrogate them, resulting in limited depth of evaluation. This aligns with the 1-3 bandâs description of âbasic argument with non-critical evaluationâ.

The student demonstrates a sound grasp of Business Management concepts such as human capital theory, investment appraisal, ROI, cost-leadership and Porterâs strategies. These are generally explained and linked to the football academy context, indicating clear but not consistently deep understanding. Some conceptual oversimplifications (e.g., equating business-volume growth directly with competitiveness) prevent a higher score, placing the work in the middle band.

Key Business Management terminologyâROI, costâbenefit analysis, investment appraisal, economies of scale, competitive advantageâis used frequently and mostly accurately. Occasional misuse (e.g., confusing revenue with profit, imprecise percentage calculations) reveals gaps in precision, and referencing of models is sometimes formulaic. The overall use is mostly accurate but not consistently precise, fitting the 3-4 band.

The essay includes basic quantitative analyses (bar charts, ROI, costâbenefit table) and applies Porterâs framework to interpret findings. Yet much of the analysis remains descriptive; calculations sometimes rely on sparse data points (e.g., only 2013 vs 2023 for ROI) and assumptions are rarely challenged. Conclusions are asserted rather than rigorously derived, limiting sophistication. This represents clear but not consistently well-supported analysis, meriting a score at the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The essay incorporates tables, charts and headline figures from Transfermarkt, annual reports and media sources, but much of the data is reproduced without rigorous interrogation. Several calculations (e.g., ROI, revenue ratios) show methodological flaws or rely on limited datapoints, and citations are occasionally vague (e.g., âVarious Sourcesâ). The application therefore shows limited appropriateness and relevance, consistent with the 1-2 band.

The methodology identifies secondary sources (financial statements, Transfermarkt, CIES, Kognity) and proposes analytical tools (ROI, cost-benefit, Porterâs strategies, Gantt chart). While these choices are generally relevant, the justification for source selection is brief and there is little critical comment on reliability or limitations beyond noting data gaps. Primary research is absent, and procedures for data collection or verification are not described in depth. Overall, the approach is serviceable but lacks the clear evidence of systematic, informed selection expected in the top band.

The student gathers a reasonable spread of financial data across ten years and supplements club reports with external databases, showing a clear link to the research question. However, source triangulation is limited and some key primary perspectives (e.g., interviews with club officials, comparative industry benchmarks) are missing. Relevance is generally clear but depth and breadth fall short of the âexcellentâ standard, justifying a score in the lower half of the 4-6 band.

Basic layout conventionsâtitle page, page numbers, and word countâare present, and images/tables are included with captions. However, formatting is uneven: some figures are poorly scaled, table borders misalign, and bibliographic entries lack a consistent citation style. The overall presentation is serviceable but lacks the accuracy and polish required for the top band, warranting a score in the 1-2 range.

The student states the topic clearly â the impact of Sporting Clube de Portugalâs academy growth on competitiveness and financial sustainability â and provides basic contextual information on the club and its relevance. Key terms such as âhuman capital theoryâ and âacademy developmentâ are introduced and linked to the business context. However, the explanation remains largely descriptive, repeating ideas rather than offering a fully developed rationale or scope. Important elements such as explicit definitions of competitiveness and sustainability measures are touched on but not comprehensively elaborated. This places the work solidly in the âadequately communicated with sufficient explanationâ range rather than the most sophisticated band.

The essay follows the required structural elements (title page, contents, introduction, body, conclusion, bibliography) and uses headings, tables and figures. Nevertheless, section ordering is occasionally confusing (e.g., Methodology split across pages), and some tables/figures are poorly formatted or interrupt the narrative flow. Referencing style is inconsistent, and captions are sometimes repetitive. These issues indicate a generally appropriate but not fully effective structure, consistent with the 1-2 band.

annotations.comment: The background provides club history and profits but does not clearly link these details to academy growthâs effect on competitiveness; refocus to emphasize relevant conceptual context.
Equating higher transfer fees to increased competitiveness oversimplifies the concept; consider integrating performance metrics (e.g., league standings) to corroborate competitive impact.
Table compares academy revenue but lacks normalization (e.g., per-player basis or club revenue ratio), reducing insight into relative performance; include normalized metrics.
Comparison with Barcelona uses absolute revenue figures without controlling for club size or market context; adjust comparisons to a common scale for validity.
The bar chart shows trend but does not report statistical measures (e.g., RÂ² value) for the trendline; include these to quantify correlation strength.
Analysis highlights fluctuations in business volume but does not explore broader causal factors (e.g., transfer market cycles); expand evaluation to improve critical depth.
Section header â2 â Revenue Streamsâ lacks preceding narrative to contextualize the figure; introduce key variables before presenting the graph to improve flow.
Fig.6 waterfall chart is informative but axis labels and units are unclear; ensure axes explicitly denote values and percent changes for reader clarity.
Word count of 3958 is stated at the beginning and under the 4000-word limit, fulfilling the formatting requirement.
The investigation title is clearly stated at the start, satisfying the formatting requirement.
The research question is clearly articulated, specifying both timeframe and dimensions (competitiveness and financial sustainability), which provides strong focus.
The table of contents comprehensively lists sections and page numbers, but consider aligning subsection indentation consistently to improve navigability.

The rationale outlines the studyâs importance but lacks precise definitions for key terms like competitiveness and financial sustainability; include these definitions to strengthen conceptual clarity.
Methodology section lists secondary sources without critical evaluation of their reliability or potential biases; add justification and limitations to demonstrate informed selection.
The introduction provides basic context but remains largely descriptive; tighten the scope by explicitly defining how human capital theory applies to academy growth.
The method describes use of secondary data but lacks detail on data collection procedures and validation processes; specify these steps to improve replicability and rigor.
Fig.1 presents club similarities but lacks analytical follow-up in the text; connect these visual comparisons directly to the research question to enhance relevance.

The approach proposes comparison of promotion rates before and after program implementation, but omits sample size and selection criteria; include this information to clarify methodological robustness.
Caption cites âtransfermarkt.pt, 2022â without access date; ensure consistent reference style by including full citations with access dates.
Discussion refers to revenue stream impacts qualitatively but omits quantitative breakdown (percentage contributions) that would substantiate claims; add these metrics.
Attributing a slight sponsorship decrease solely to external agreements lacks evidence; consider exploring sponsorship portfolio changes to support assertions.
Fig.7 only plots start and end values; include intermediate yearly data to illustrate the full evolution of cost and value over the decade.
ROI formula is presented without clear parentheses, risking misinterpretation; rewrite as $$ROI=\frac{1.5\text{Mâ¬}-0.05\text{Mâ¬}}{0.05\text{Mâ¬}}\times100\%$$ and explain each term.
Promotion rate calculation assumes consistent academy size; justify this assumption or adjust the formula using actual historical team B numbers.
The Gantt chart is detailed but lacks a clear legend explaining timeline units and bar meanings; add a legend and axis labels for better comprehension.
Mathematical expression uses HTML <math> tags; switch to LaTeX syntax (e.g., $$\frac{1}{3}$$) to maintain consistency and readability.

Porterâs cost-leadership argument refers to low costs broadly but does not link specific academy cost metrics to industry benchmarks; incorporate comparative cost data to substantiate this strategy.
Fig.9 citation lists 'dachpian' without full bibliographic details; provide complete source information and confirm usage permissions.
Cost-benefit methodology is outlined but benefits and costs remain generic; tailor categories to the clubâs financial data and justify impact ratings.
The cost-benefit table is densely formatted; consider splitting into separate cost and benefit tables or reducing column widths to improve readability.
Bibliography entries lack a consistent citation style; apply a uniform format (e.g., APA or MLA) and alphabetize entries for clarity.
Stray URL fragment appears without context; remove or relocate this text to maintain a polished bibliography.
Reflection on limitations is descriptive; deepen analysis by evaluating how data gaps influenced your conclusions and what alternative approaches could mitigate these issues.
The conclusion summarizes findings but could further integrate theoretical implications and recommend actionable strategies for club management based on your analysis.
